title: Build the Agent Commons public workbench
objective: Let people and fresh-context Agents discover, execute, verify, and hand off independently valuable public checkpoints without relying on private chat memory.
scope: Versioned task, continuation, deliberation, recognition, entry-surface, and validation contracts
GitHub Issue and Project projections for the public workbench
non_goals: No Agent or model ranking, automatic permission, automatic merge, or private reasoning transcript
No package implementation or device-support promotion

checkpoints.title: Define task checkpoint and continuation contracts
checkpoints.outcome: Machine-readable checkpoint, lease, execution-anchor, continuation, live-registry, and capability-routing contracts are public and reproducible.
non_goals: No package source, Device Lab artifact implementation, repository permission, or workflow change

required_capabilities: JSON Schema 2020-12 and state-machine contract design
Git and GitHub branch, pull-request, evidence, and lease semantics
required_tools: GitHub CLI with read/write access held by the accountable maintainer
qualification_evidence: Passing authority, task, continuation, and registry schema validation at the checkpoint revision

acceptance.criterion: Every checkpoint records dependencies, bounded paths, routing, acceptance, verification, evidence, lease, anchor, and exact next action.
The live registry distinguishes versioned execution authority from Issue coordination and Project discovery projections.
A fresh-context Agent can resume admitted work from public state without a private session.

verification.procedure: Validate the Task Hall authority, task example, continuation example, registry, and registered live task against their declared JSON Schemas.
verification.expected: Every declared instance passes at the published contract commit; repository-validator integration is delegated to WB-01V.

evidence.summary: The exact public commit passed five JSON Schema/instance checks, a bounded-path audit, a package-diff check, and a public leakage scan in a clean detached worktree.
The maintainer accepted the exact public checkpoint only after clean-commit validation evidence was posted; validator integration remained delegated to the independently reviewed WB-01V checkpoint.

claim.transition_reason: The WB-01 contract boundary was published and independently revalidated from its exact clean commit; validator integration continues as WB-01V.

checkpoints.title: Bind the validator to the public task registry
checkpoints.outcome: The repository validator and focused tests enforce Task Hall 0.3.0, live-registry projection, and capability-routing invariants without absorbing later fan-in checks.
non_goals: No contract-document, package, Device Lab, workflow, permission, WB-02 recognition, or WB-04 remote-evidence change
do_not_touch: WB-04 remote reachability, stale-anchor, evidence-blob, and review fan-in

required_capabilities: Python validator design for JSON Schema and cross-file invariants
Can preserve an exact write boundary while distinguishing contract validation from later integration review
required_tools: Python 3, jsonschema, Git, and the repository contract-test runner
qualification_evidence: Positive fixtures plus fail-closed missing, duplicate, foreign-repository, and unsafe-routing mutations at the checkpoint revision

acceptance.criterion: Registry paths, ids, states, umbrella Issues, canonical repository, and unique checkpoint-to-Issue projections are cross-file consistent.
Missing projections, duplicate Issue projections, foreign repositories, self-authorizing routing, and unqualified high-risk execution fail closed.
A fresh-context Cursor implementation uses only the exact WB-01 public commit and two allowed files, then receives independent Codex review.

verification.procedure: Run focused registry/projection/routing tests, the full repository contract suite, the repository validator, and an exact write-boundary audit.
verification.expected: All positive and negative checks pass with changes limited to the two allowed files.

evidence.summary: Six independent adversarial review rounds closed registry, routing, path-containment, portable ownership-key, and fail-closed resolution gaps. The exact head passed the repository validator, 102 contract tests with one Windows symlink-capability skip, and GitHub Actions before merge as ccbc5bd0a1457b0c6b6def56b5100832672a8509.

claim.transition_reason: The independently reviewed validator head was merged into the Agent Commons integration branch and the merged-tree readback passed.

checkpoints.title: Publish the public-workbench RFC and recognition contract
checkpoints.outcome: The public RFC separates source facts from project decisions and keeps contribution evidence, recognition, and repository authority as distinct ledgers.
non_goals: No points, total score, Agent/model ranking, or automatic role promotion

required_capabilities: Can distinguish recognition from trust and permission
required_tools: Repository validator and public source access
qualification_evidence: Source-fact/inference audit and independent review of authority claims

acceptance.criterion: Upstream facts and XR Foundry design inferences are visibly separated.
Recognition never implies quality, trust, permission, reviewer status, or maintainership.

verification.procedure: Run source-manifest, credit-contract, privacy, and repository validation plus independent source-boundary review.
verification.expected: All references resolve and no recognition record grants authority.

evidence.summary: The public-workbench RFC, admitted-source manifest, non-ranking recognition policy, and fail-closed contribution-credit contract are published and pass repository validation.
The repository maintainer approved the public source-fact, recognition, authority, and continuation boundary while preserving WB-04 validation and deferred device gates.

claim.transition_reason: The maintainer approved the reviewed WB-02 boundary and released the completed checkpoint to WB-03 and WB-04 fan-in.

checkpoints.title: Expose one clear contributor and Agent entry route
checkpoints.outcome: Repository, Issue forms, pull-request template, and Project expose one coherent discovery-to-handoff route.
non_goals: No package, workflow-permission, secret, or automatic external Agent authority change

required_capabilities: Fresh-context contributor onboarding and GitHub Project projection audit
required_tools: GitHub read access and repository link checker
qualification_evidence: A public fresh-context route audit covering repository, Issue, Project, PR, and handoff links

acceptance.criterion: A newcomer or fresh-context Agent can find one bounded claim key, authority boundary, and exact next action without private chat.
Project fields are documented and implemented as discovery summaries, not fine-grained machine authority.

verification.procedure: Audit every entry link and compare Issue, Project, and registered task projections.
verification.expected: All links resolve and projection roles are consistent.

evidence.summary: The repository guide, contributor route, Agent entry, Issue/PR templates, and GitHub playbook expose one public checkpoint-to-handoff path without private session state.
The repository maintainer approved the public newcomer and Agent entry boundary after WB-02 completion, without granting external write or merge authority.

claim.transition_reason: The lease was admitted before WB-01 and WB-02 completed and was released to restore the dependency gate.

checkpoints.title: Record shared deliberation without ranking Agents
checkpoints.outcome: A lightweight schema and protocol preserve non-binding proposals, revisable synthesis, current decisions, and delta-only challenges without private chain-of-thought.
non_goals: No Agent/model scoring, private session transcript, or automatic execution authority
do_not_touch: Task Hall or RFC integration text owned by sibling checkpoints

required_capabilities: Mechanism design for revisable public decisions and bounded reasoning summaries
Can distinguish deliberation, synthesis, decision, execution, and authority
required_tools: JSON Schema validator and public Discussion read access
qualification_evidence: One resolved and one unresolved record that preserve counterexamples, reopen conditions, and exact next actions

acceptance.criterion: Later contributors can add evidence or counterexample deltas without making the first proposal binding.
Resolved and unresolved examples distinguish current decision, open questions, reopen conditions, and exact next action.

verification.procedure: Validate both examples against the schema and review them from a fresh context.
verification.expected: The resolved example is executable, the unresolved example remains non-executable, and neither contains model ranking fields.

evidence.summary: Open and resolved records pass Draft 2020-12 validation; four contradictory terminal-state fixtures fail closed; an independent Cursor advisory review found and the follow-up commit closed rejected-ready and superseded-ready gaps.
The repository maintainer approved the separation of deliberation, synthesis, decision, execution, and authority while retaining versioned adoption and WB-04 adversarial validation.

claim.transition_reason: The maintainer approved the reviewed WB-05 deliberation boundary and released the completed checkpoint to WB-04 fan-in.

checkpoints.title: Enforce and independently review public-workbench invariants
checkpoints.outcome: Validators and adversarial tests reject contradictory, stale, unreachable, self-authorizing, or privacy-unsafe public-workbench states.
non_goals: No package implementation, repository permission, secret, or self-acceptance change
do_not_touch: Acceptance by the executor whose output is under review

required_capabilities: Adversarial validator design, Git/GitHub reachability, and public-workbench state-machine review
required_tools: Python 3, Git, GitHub read access, and repository validator
qualification_evidence: Positive and negative fixtures for dependency, lease, continuation, fork PR, evidence, privacy, and authority boundaries

acceptance.criterion: Valid dependency, handoff, fork-PR, reclaim/resume, and public-anchor paths pass.
Cycles, stale digests, expired leases, self-granted authority, path escape, identity mismatch, and unsupported claims fail closed.

verification.procedure: Run the full repository validator, Python contract suite, leakage scan, and an independent terminal review.
verification.expected: Every check passes with no unresolved high-risk finding.

evidence.summary: The exact public implementation passed repository validation, the 108-test contract suite with one Windows symlink-capability skip, leakage checks, and GitHub Actions.

A fresh-context read-only Cursor review found three gaps in the prior revision; two were resolved and the shared-maintainer Agent boundary was made explicit before the exact remediation commit received a PASS.

claim.transition_reason: The exact public remediation commit passed the full local gate and GitHub Actions, and an independent Cursor Agent confirmed all requested findings closed.

integration.strategy: An independent reviewer reproduces all checkpoint evidence, and the maintainer decides whether PR #46 can merge.
